analyst.question: Will BTC reach a new all-time high in 2026?

reasoning.text: The market prices YES at 0.160, implying only a 16% probability BTC sets a new all-time high at any point during calendar year 2026. With ~7 months remaining in the resolution window (June–December 2026), this is an extreme discount for a historically cyclical asset in a post-halving year.
Liquidity is deep at $540,000 and 24h volume is over $1.2M — far above the $25k hard-pass threshold. Slippage is not a concern; edge can be captured meaningfully here.

BTC historically enters strong bull phases in the 12–24 months following a halving event. May 2026 places us well within that typical window. The structural supply shock from the most recent halving compresses sell-side pressure, and institutional demand channels (ETF inflows, corporate treasury allocation) remain active catalysts throughout 2026.

A 16% probability for ANY single daily close above the prior ATH across ~7 remaining months is deeply inconsistent with BTC's realized volatility profile. Even in choppy regimes, BTC regularly produces multi-month rallies of 40–80%+. The threshold here is a single daily close above one prior peak — not a sustained breakout. This is a low bar across a long window.

Key risks that could suppress YES: macro deterioration (aggressive Fed re-tightening, credit shock, risk-off flight), crypto-specific regulatory crackdown (e.g., US stablecoin or spot ETF restrictions), or a prolonged consolidation phase where BTC grinds sideways through year-end. These are real but do not collectively justify an 84% NO probability.

Surveying base rates: in post-halving years historically, BTC has reached new ATHs in 3 of the 3 prior cycles within 18 months of the halving. While past cycles don't guarantee outcomes, the structural setup (ETF demand, macro liquidity easing bias, corporate adoption) broadly supports continuation. A fair probability of new ATH in 2026 is closer to 65–75%.

Fair price YES = ~0.72. Market is pricing YES at 0.16 — a massive 56-percentage-point gap. Edge is approximately +5,600 bps before slippage, well above the 200 bps threshold. Liquidity supports the position. BUY YES with 68% confidence.
